public_reputation: Known locally through day-to-day work as law speaker.
appearance: Pela March carries the marks of law speaker in ink, rain, and polished cloth: practical wear, maintained details, and a look that suggests they expect to be read before they are trusted.
manner: Measures every exchange for precedent, alignment, and the cost of appearing uncertain. In The Union, that reads as someone deal-aware, balanced, and procedurally diplomatic, with an eye on leverage and precedent, and the habits of a law speaker still shape how they handle timing, silence, and attention.
daily_function: Performs the ongoing duties of law speaker in and around House Mal, where the role has practical consequences even when nothing dramatic is happening.
current_agenda: Protect their footing in House Mal while steering current strain away from a public rupture they may not survive politically or materially.
hidden_pressure: A private obligation, debt, promise, or fear tied to reputational exposure is narrowing their options faster than they admit.

adventure_hooks.summary: Pela March needs discreet outside help to contain political strain, prestige loss, or institutional damage before it spreads through House Mal.
Getting entangled with Pela March can pull the party into protocol, alliance pressure, or public consequence centered on House Mal.
Pela March can trade usable insight about House Mal if the party solves a problem that cannot be addressed openly through the office they hold.

support_scope_notes: Promoted from a preexisting named interface into the unified full-content NPC architecture. Sparse unsupported fields remain intentionally restrained. Appearance extended under V33_1 from role/locality/public-signal derivation rather than decorative fabrication.
public_signal: Dress quality, attention from others, and practiced command of protocol signal that Pela March carries civic or noble weight.

encounter_context.usual_environment: House Mal: estates, audience spaces, council rooms, protocol-heavy gatherings, or civic seats

first_impression: Pela March presents as Measures every exchange for precedent, alignment, and the cost of appearing uncertain. In The Union, that reads as someone deal-aware, balanced, and procedurally diplomatic, with an eye on leverage and precedent, and the habits of a law speaker still shape how they handle timing, silence, and attention. and immediately reads for leverage, obligation, and risk.
what_they_want_from_strangers: Useful clarity, respect for Law Speaker, and proof that strangers will not create avoidable noise in The Union.
what_they_refuse: Pela March refuses careless disrespect, vague promises, and demands that threaten standing or workload.
how_they_create_problems: By enforcing Law Speaker too rigidly, protecting reputation, or turning a small request into a larger obligation.
how_they_can_help: Pela March can open doors, explain local expectations, or redirect trouble before it reaches a harder authority layer.
escalation_if_crossed: If crossed, Pela March adds delay, scrutiny, and reputational drag before direct punishment.
rumor_about_them: People say Pela March is Known locally through day-to-day work as law speaker. and rarely acts without a second motive.
best_first_scene: A first encounter works best when the party needs guidance, access, or mercy from a Law Speaker.
social_texture: Pela March speaks like someone who has spent too long translating private stakes into acceptable public language. Around House Mal, people read them as socially alert, procedural, and sensitive to status.
money_pressure: Their material pressure is tied to favors and tariffs; even when wealthy, they think in terms of loss, leverage, and what one bad week would expose.
status_anxiety: What they fear most is not simple failure but reputational exposure becoming socially visible before they can contain it.
long_memory: They remember who made their work harder, who preserved dignity under strain, and who tried to force speed without bearing consequence.
obvious_tell: When pressure rises, Pela touches clothing, tools, or insignia before answering, as if checking that their role is still intact.
best_use_case: Use Pela March when the party needs a socially grounded face for House Mal: access, warning, delay, price, or reluctant help.
private_contradiction: They want to appear consistent, but their real choices are split between duty, self-protection, and preserving one relationship they cannot afford to lose.
